preflopChart.default: Defend enough, but do it with hands that realize equity. Do not drift into garbage offsuit bluff-catchers.
preflopChart.continueHands: 22+, A2s+, A8o+, K7s+, KTo+, Q8s+, QTo+, J8s+, JTo, T8s+, 98s-54s
preflopChart.threeBetHands: QQ+, AK for value. Mix in A5s-A4s, KQs at some frequency when opener overfolds.
preflopChart.foldHands: K6o-, Q9o-, J9o-, T8o-, 97o-, weak offsuit trash like 94o/84o/74o

preflopChart.default: Continue wider than versus other positions, but still avoid ego-defense with hands that make dominated one pair.
preflopChart.threeBetHands: TT+, AQ+, A5s-A2s, KJs+, QJs when villain opens too wide / folds too much.
preflopChart.foldHands: Most offsuit junk below Q9o, weak king-rag offsuit, hands like Q3o/J4o/T5o/95o

preflopChart.default: Tighten up. Fewer optimistic peels, more discipline.
preflopChart.continueHands: 22+, A2s+, ATo+, K9s+, KJo+, Q9s+, QJo, J9s+, T8s+, 98s-65s
preflopChart.foldHands: QTo borderline, most offsuit connectors/gappers, most weak suited trash that plays poorly OOP

preflopChart.default: Prefer 3-bet or fold over flatting a bunch of medium-strength hands out of position.
preflopChart.continueHands: If flatting, keep it strong and suited: 22-99, A2s-AJs, KTs+, QTs+, JTs, T9s-76s
preflopChart.foldHands: KJo-/QJo-/QTo/JTo type hands that feel playable but create ugly postflop spots

preflopChart.default: When unsure in the blinds, fold the offsuit junk and keep the suited connected hands that can make nuttier outcomes.
preflopChart.continueHands: Suited aces, suited broadways, pocket pairs, good suited connectors
preflopChart.threeBetHands: Premiums plus a few wheel-ace bluffs
preflopChart.foldHands: Marginal offsuit broadways, weak king-x offsuit, random one-gappers with poor realization

avoidList.group: Hands to stop fucking with from the blinds versus opens
avoidList.why: These hands realize terribly, make dominated one pair, and create the exact medium-pot bleed we want gone.

avoidList.group: Borderline flats out of position that should mostly become folds or 3-bets
avoidList.hands: KJo, KTo, QTo, JTo, KJs in some formations, QJo from SB/BB versus tighter opens
avoidList.why: These hands look pretty but often turn into weak bluff-catchers or hopeful floats.

avoidList.hands: A2o-A9o blind defense versus most positions, especially CO/MP opens
avoidList.why: Blockers are not enough. They get dominated and tempt you into sticky top-pair mistakes.

avoidList.hands: J8s, T8s, 97s, 86s, 75s when OOP versus solid opens unless price and opponent make it clear
avoidList.why: These are fine sometimes, but you currently over-realize the upside and underweight how often they just bleed.

avoidList.hands: Any hand where your only reason to continue is 'it feels defendable'
avoidList.why: That sentence is usually the warning light, not the green light.

topHands.title: KK into AA preflop all-in
topHands.takeaway: Pure cooler. Important because it drove the session result, but this is not the hand to study for leak reduction.

topHands.title: ATo in a 3-bet pot
topHands.takeaway: Calm line. Called a reasonable c-bet, stayed under control, and value-bet river once checked to. Good template for medium-strength showdown hands.

topHands.title: 88 river bet/fold
topHands.takeaway: Good discipline. One-pair hand got value and did not pay off a strong river raise.

topHands.title: QTo blind defend and hopeful turn stab
topHands.takeaway: This is a target leak. Marginal preflop continue followed by a stab just because villain checked once.

topHands.title: KJs flat and flop peel drift
topHands.takeaway: Classic medium-pot bleed. Pretty hand, weak realization, ends up losing at showdown with king high.

topHands.title: 94o defend versus button open
topHands.takeaway: Hand class to eliminate. This is exactly the kind of defend that slowly buries winrate.

topHands.title: AJo disciplined fold on third diamond
topHands.takeaway: You avoided letting one top-pair hand snowball into a bigger mistake. Good example of shutting it down once the line gets uglier.

topHands.title: AKcc bluff-catch threshold spot
topHands.takeaway: Worth studying closely. Likely near the line between mandatory continue and over-defending based on pool tendencies.

topHands.title: QTo on an ace-high paired texture
topHands.takeaway: Another example of staying involved too long with a hand class that creates optimistic postflop decisions.

topHands.title: JJ on A-5-5-K-A
topHands.takeaway: Useful river discipline hand. Good fold, but worth reviewing sizing and whether flop c-bet is best versus that pool/player.

utg_leaks.verdict: LEAK — ATo not in UTG range (mix is AJo only). Fold preflop.

bb_defend_leaks.verdict: LEAK — on explicit avoid list. Fold vs all opens.
LEAK — weak offsuit ace, dominated OOP. Fold vs UTG/MP.

key_leaks: UTG range discipline: opened ATo (non-mix hand)
BB defend trash: 94o x2 and A8o — combined -$18.5
C-bet fold equity near zero (4.3%) — opponents not folding; stop bluffing unimproved
3-bet frequency too low (0.9%) — missing steal defence EV
sessionAnalysis.study_priority: BB defense range discipline + texture-based c-betting strategy

utg_leaks.verdict: CLEAN — QJs is in UTG range. Postflop loss (-$120) not a preflop error.

bb_defend_leaks.verdict: LEAK — offsuit wheel ace, terrible OOP. Fold.
LEAK — bottom-of-range suited connector, too weak vs any reasonable open.

key_leaks: BB defend range continues to bleed with off-chart hands (A2o, 83s)
0% 3-bet rate — pattern from session 1 continuing; need squeeze spots and blind defence 3-bets
C-bet fold equity still 0% — opponents are sticky; adjust to smaller sizes or x/b approach
QJh trips call-off: run through solver — likely correct call with trips but worth checking pot odds (corrected: 29-33% required equity)
sessionAnalysis.study_priority: 3-bet frequency + BB defense range vs each position + pot odds on triple barrel spots
